target.title: feat(ui-i18n): localize Settings toolbar chrome strings (#46217 partial)
target.body:
## Summary

Phase 1 of #46217: replaces 20 hardcoded English strings in the Settings view toolbar with `t()` calls under a new `settings.*` namespace in `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts`. Runs `pnpm ui:i18n:sync` to regenerate all 18 locale bundles. `zh-CN` and `zh-TW` ship with native-speaker translations (seeded into the TM so they survive future sync runs); other locales fall through to English fallback per the existing no-provider behavior.

Strings covered (toolbar chrome only):
- Mode toggle: `Form`, `Raw`, plus the two toggle tooltips and `Raw mode unavailable for this snapshot`
- Changes badge: `Unsaved changes` (raw mode), `{count} unsaved change` / `{count} unsaved changes` (form mode, sing/plural — fix applied after @clawsweeper review), `No changes`
- Action buttons: `Open`, `Open config file`, `Open {path}` tooltip, `Clear`, `Apply` / `Applying…`, `Update` / `Updating…`
- Search: placeholder, aria-label, clear-search aria-label
- Accordion: `Quick Settings` header
- `Raw mode disabled (snapshot cannot safely round-trip raw text).` notice

The `Save` / `Saving…` button reuses existing `common.save` / `common.saving` keys (which already carry native translations across all locales — `保存` / `保存中…` in zh-CN, `儲存` / `儲存中…` in zh-TW). The sing/plural pair `unsavedChangeCount` / `unsavedChangesCount` follows the repo's existing pattern (`common.checkpoint` / `common.checkpoints`).

## Out of scope (separate PRs to follow)

Per the @clawsweeper review on #46217, the full localization touches three additional surfaces that intentionally are NOT in this PR:

- `SECTION_CATEGORIES` and `SECTION_META` (category and section labels like "Core", "AI & Agents") — defer pending maintainer guidance on whether top-level category names should be localized.
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` `FIELD_LABELS` — needs a locale-aware API contract change, which the review explicitly says "needs maintainer product/API direction before automation".
- Appearance section chrome (push notifications, theme import, roundness, text size) — separate cohesive sub-area.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Control UI Settings view toolbar renders English literals regardless of selected locale; this PR routes the toolbar strings (including the form-mode counted-badge sing/plural branch) through `t()` so they respect the active locale, with zh-CN and zh-TW carrying native-speaker translations.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout, node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.1.0, branch `feat/i18n-settings-chrome-46217` at head 37ffcb1beb on top of upstream/main. Vite dev server started with `pnpm ui:dev` (port 5173) and a real Chrome browser session loaded the regenerated `zh-CN` and `zh-TW` locale bundles via the running dev server, including the new form-mode count keys added after the @clawsweeper review.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
pnpm ui:i18n:sync
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm ui:dev
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/config.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/config-form.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/config-form.search.node.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts ui/src/ui/views/config.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
```

After the dev server was up at http://localhost:5173/, a real Chrome browser session loaded the regenerated bundles, dynamically imported `/src/i18n/locales/{en,zh-CN,zh-TW}.ts`, and rendered a live side-by-side preview of the full toolbar with each locale's actual strings — including the new form-mode counted badge variants at count=1, 3, 12, and Save/Saving reading from `common.save` / `common.saving`.

Evidence after fix:

Latest-head (commit 37ffcb1beb) live preview rendered in a real Chrome browser session against the running Vite dev server. Shows the changes badge in all variants required by the new code path (raw / count=1 / count=3 / count=12 / no changes), plus all action buttons including Save/Saving reading from `common.save` / `common.saving`. All three locales side-by-side using the regenerated bundles:

![Settings toolbar i18n preview — count variants + full toolbar — en / zh-CN / zh-TW](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ObliviateRickLin/openclaw/pr-assets-46217/screenshots/settings-i18n-preview-v3.png)

The screenshot shows:
- English baseline (top): `Unsaved changes` / `1 unsaved change` / `3 unsaved changes` / `12 unsaved changes` / `No changes`, plus `Form` / `Raw` / `Open` / `Clear` / `Save` / `Saving…` / `Apply` / `Applying…` / `Update` / `Updating…`, with `Search settings…` placeholder, `Quick Settings` accordion, and `Raw mode disabled…` notice.
- Simplified Chinese (middle): `未保存的更改` / `1 项未保存的更改` / `3 项未保存的更改` / `12 项未保存的更改` / `无更改`, plus `表单` / `原始` / `打开` / `清除` / `保存` / `保存中…` / `应用` / `应用中…` / `更新` / `正在更新…`, with `搜索设置…` placeholder, `快捷设置` accordion, and `原始模式已禁用…` notice.
- Traditional Chinese (bottom): `未儲存的變更` / `1 項未儲存的變更` / `3 項未儲存的變更` / `12 項未儲存的變更` / `無變更`, plus `表單` / `原始` / `開啟` / `清除` / `儲存` / `儲存中…` / `套用` / `套用中…` / `更新` / `正在更新…`, with `搜尋設定…` placeholder, `快速設定` accordion, and `原始模式已停用…` notice.

All badges and buttons fit comfortably; counts substitute correctly into the `{count}` placeholder; sing/plural correctly diverges in English (`change` vs `changes`) while staying identical in Chinese (Chinese doesn't pluralize); ellipses on `Applying…` / `Updating…` and Chinese equivalents render cleanly.

Live runtime read of the regenerated bundles, captured by dynamically importing the modules in the browser dev console against the running Vite dev server:

```
en.settings.unsavedChangeCount       -> "{count} unsaved change"
en.settings.unsavedChangesCount      -> "{count} unsaved changes"
zh_CN.settings.unsavedChangeCount    -> "{count} 项未保存的更改"
zh_CN.settings.unsavedChangesCount   -> "{count} 项未保存的更改"
zh_TW.settings.unsavedChangeCount    -> "{count} 項未儲存的變更"
zh_TW.settings.unsavedChangesCount   -> "{count} 項未儲存的變更"

en.common.save        -> "Save"
en.common.saving      -> "Saving…"
zh_CN.common.save     -> "保存"
zh_CN.common.saving   -> "保存中…"
zh_TW.common.save     -> "儲存"
zh_TW.common.saving   -> "儲存中…"
```

Real `pnpm ui:i18n:check` console output (post-patch, locally on the branch):

```
control-ui-i18n: raw-copy: baseline entries=646
zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0)
zh-TW: clean (fallbacks=0)
pt-BR: clean (fallbacks=20)
de:    clean (fallbacks=20)
... 16 more locales reporting clean
```

`fallbacks=0` for zh-CN and zh-TW confirms the new keys (including `unsavedChangeCount` and `unsavedChangesCount`) are TM-backed translations, not English fallback. Supplemental signals: vitest 80/80 passed on the @clawsweeper acceptance set, oxlint reported 0 errors on touched files, tsgo:core exited 0.

Observed result after fix: `ui/src/ui/views/config.ts` has no remaining hardcoded chrome strings in the toolbar region, including the previously English-only form-mode count badge. `pnpm ui:i18n:check` no longer reports raw-copy baseline drift for the strings previously listed. `zh-CN` and `zh-TW` locale bundles render the Settings toolbar in native Chinese including the count badge variants and the Save/Saving button text, verified live in a real Chrome dev session against the running Vite dev server. TM entries seeded for both Chinese locales (all 22 new settings.* keys) so the translations persist across future maintainer sync runs.

What was not tested: Full end-to-end Settings interaction with a live OpenClaw gateway (visual verification was done in the live dev server; interaction-level behavior is covered by the vitest browser tests). Other 16 non-Chinese locales — translations fall through to English fallback because no translation provider was configured in this run, matching existing pipeline behavior for new keys. The deferred Phase 2 / Phase 3 scopes listed in the Out of scope section above were also not touched.

target.title: fix(infra): bound ClawHub fetchJson and error response bodies
target.body:
## Summary

ClawHub is an external marketplace and therefore an **untrusted source**. Every
ClawHub response reader in `src/infra/clawhub.ts` previously consumed the body
without a byte cap, so a hostile or malfunctioning ClawHub host could exhaust
process memory by streaming an unbounded response:

- `fetchJson` (shared by search/detail/version/artifact-resolver/verify/security
  metadata) called `await response.json()` — the entire success body was
  buffered before parsing, with no upper bound.
- `readErrorBody` called `await response.text()` — the entire error body was
  buffered into the diagnostic/error message, with no upper bound.
- `fetchClawHubSkillInstallResolution` called a bare `await response.json()` for
  both the OK and the structured 403/409/410/423 install bodies — the one
  remaining uncapped ClawHub JSON reader.

This is the symmetric, harder-justified counterpart to the error-stream
hardening landed in #95108 (which bounded the Anthropic Messages error stream):
here the source is a third-party marketplace, not first-party transport.

## Changes

- `fetchJson` now delegates to a small `parseClawHubJsonBody` helper that reads
  the success body through the existing `readResponseWithLimit` helper under a
  16 MiB cap (`CLAWHUB_JSON_MAX_BYTES`), then decodes and `JSON.parse`s the
  bounded buffer. Overflow and idle-timeout cancel the stream (releasing the
  socket/buffer) and throw a descriptive error; malformed JSON keeps throwing
  the existing `returned malformed JSON` error.
- `readErrorBody` now uses `readResponseTextSnippet` (the same helper #95108
  used) under an 8 KiB byte cap and 400-char display cap, collapsing whitespace
  and truncating with `…`. The `statusText` / `HTTP <status>` fallback chain is
  preserved.
- `fetchClawHubSkillInstallResolution` now **reuses the same
  `parseClawHubJsonBody` helper** instead of its own bare `response.json()`, so
  every ClawHub JSON success/structured-block body is bounded by the identical
  cap. This is pure reuse of the helper introduced in this PR — no new
  abstraction — and closes the last uncapped ClawHub JSON path that the review
  flagged.
- Regression tests cover: an oversized 200 JSON body via `searchClawHubSkills`
  (asserts the cap error + stream cancellation), an oversized error body
  (asserts the message is collapsed/truncated and stays far below the raw body),
  and an oversized install-resolution body via
  `fetchClawHubSkillInstallResolution` (asserts the cap error + cancellation).

Out of scope (intentionally focused): the `MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` default on
`readClawHubResponseBytes` for artifact/archive *downloads* — those are length-
checked binary fetches with their own integrity handling, not in-memory JSON
parses, and belong in a separate change.

## Why this is worth merging

- **Untrusted-source memory-exhaustion vector.** ClawHub is an external
  marketplace; an unbounded `response.json()`/`response.text()` lets a hostile or
  malfunctioning host stream an arbitrarily large body straight into process
  memory. The narrowest maintainable fix is to route reads through the existing
  response-limit helper, which is exactly what this does.
- **Complete, not scoped.** A prior review noted one sibling ClawHub JSON path
  (`fetchClawHubSkillInstallResolution`) was still uncapped; it is now bounded
  through the same helper, so there is no remaining direct `response.json()` /
  `response.text()` on any ClawHub response path.

## How the `merge-risk: availability` is bounded

The label flags that this changes response-body reading for live ClawHub
requests. The change is **purely additive on the success path**: the bounded
reader streams chunks and only diverges from prior behaviour when a body exceeds
16 MiB (JSON) / 8 KiB (error snippet). Real ClawHub responses are kilobytes
(a live `?q=calendar&limit=5` search is ~2 KB), orders of magnitude under the
cap, so normal traffic parses byte-for-byte as before — verified live below.
Beyond the cap, the previous behaviour was *unbounded buffering* (the actual
availability/OOM risk); the new behaviour is a fast, descriptive failure with
the stream cancelled. So the cap narrows an availability risk rather than adding
one.

## Real behavior proof

A local tsx harness (`proof.mts`) drove the **real** exported
`searchClawHubSkills` and `fetchClawHubSkillInstallResolution` (no mocks of the
code under test). For injected cases it streams an oversized body through a
`ReadableStream` whose `cancel()` hook is observed, so a `cancelled: true`
readback proves the production code released the stream instead of draining it.
For the normal path it hits the **live clawhub.ai marketplace** over the network.

- **Behavior addressed**: unbounded buffering of untrusted ClawHub JSON and
  error response bodies (memory-exhaustion / availability vector).
- **Real environment tested**: Node v22.22.0, `tsx`, against the patched
  `src/infra/clawhub.ts`; vitest via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`.
- **Exact steps / commands run after this patch**:
  - `./node_modules/.bin/tsx proof.mts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  - **LIVE normal path** (real network, `searchClawHubSkills` →
    live `https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/search?q=calendar`): `HTTP 200`,
    `5 skill(s)` parsed through the bounded `parseClawHubJsonBody`
    (`first.slug = calendar`, `displayName` present). Production JSON parses
    correctly after `response.json()` was replaced by the bounded reader.
  - **fetchJson cap** (injected 17 MiB): threw
    `ClawHub /api/v1/search response exceeded 16777216 bytes (17301504 bytes received)`,
    `stream cancelled: true`.
  - **install-resolution cap** (injected 17 MiB, sibling path): threw
    `ClawHub /api/v1/skills/weather/install response exceeded 16777216 bytes (17301504 bytes received)`,
    `stream cancelled: true`.
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 55 passed | 2 skipped (57)`
    (the 2 skips are pre-existing live-network-gated tests).
- **Observed result after fix**: live production JSON parses unchanged; oversized
  bodies on both the shared and the install-resolution JSON paths stop at the
  cap and cancel the underlying stream.
- **Negative control**: the harness re-runs the *pre-fix* behaviour — an
  unbounded `Response.arrayBuffer()` drain of the same 17 MiB stream. It reads
  the full `17825792` bytes (~17 MiB), `stream cancelled: false`, and never
  stops early. This confirms the cap is load-bearing: without it the stream is
  fully buffered into memory, with it the reader cancels at 16 MiB.
- **What was not tested**: a live *oversized* ClawHub response (the live host
  returns small bodies; oversized behaviour is proven with injected streams that
  drive the same production reader), and the out-of-scope artifact-download
  default cap noted above.

AI-assisted (human-run real behavior proof included).

target.title: Feature/pr 21b normal mode startup channel backoff
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: normal-mode gateway startup could appear opaque or indefinitely delayed when channel startup was slow, failed, or deferred behind sidecar startup work.
- Why it matters: operators need startup-doctor/runtime-state output that clearly distinguishes HTTP readiness from full startup readiness, and slow/failing channels should not make startup diagnostics misleading.
- What changed: added startup runtime-state reporting, startup-doctor readiness semantics, per-channel startup result tracking, timeout/backoff handling, sanitized runtime-state errors/warnings, and focused gateway tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider config changes, no UI changes, no queue/security-policy changes, no installed package patching, and no runtime config/secrets included.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

- Behavior or issue addressed: normal-mode startup diagnostics did not clearly report channel startup outcomes, deferred sidecar readiness, or full-readiness state.
- Real environment tested: Windows development checkout on branch `feature/pr-21b-normal-mode-startup-channel-backoff`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git status --short
git branch --show-current
git log --oneline -3
git diff --name-only main...HEAD
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/startup-runtime-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts
```
 - Evidence after fix:
 ```bash
git diff --check: passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/startup-runtime-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts

2 test files passed
40 tests passed
```

 - Observed result after fix:
    - runtime-state strings redact secret-like values before truncation
    - startup-doctor reports explicit readiness fields
    - immediate channel startup rejection is counted as failed
    - later channels continue starting after one channel fails
channel timeout cases are reported as timed_out
 - What was not tested:
    - full repo typecheck could not complete due Node/V8 heap/runtime crash, including with 8GB heap
    - live external channel startup was not exercised against real third-party services
 - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    - prior focused QA observed channelsStarted: 2 and channelsFailed: 0 when one channel startup rejected; this PR now tests and fixes the expected 1/1 started/failed split.
## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write N/A. If the cause is unclear, write Unknown.

 - Root cause: channel startup handoff could be treated as successful before immediate startup-time rejection was observed, and startup runtime-state output did not previously provide enough structured readiness/channel outcome detail.
 - Missing detection / guardrail: there were no focused tests locking in immediate channel startup rejection classification, runtime-state redaction idempotency, or explicit full-readiness semantics.
 - Contributing context (if known): normal-mode startup includes deferred sidecar/channel work, which can make HTTP availability and full gateway readiness diverge.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write N/A.

 - Coverage level that should have caught this:
   -  [ ] Unit test
   -  [ ] Seam / integration test
   -  [ ] End-to-end test
   -  [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
 - Target test or file:
    - src/gateway/startup-runtime-state.test.ts
    - src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts
 - Scenario the test should lock in:
    - sensitive runtime-state strings are redacted before truncation
    - redaction is idempotent
    - channel startup success is counted as started
    - immediate channel startup rejection is counted as failed
    - later channels still start after one failure
    - channel startup timeout is counted as timed_out
 - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
    - these focused gateway tests isolate startup-state and channel-startup behavior without requiring real external services or user runtime config.
 - Existing test that already covers this (if any):
    - new/updated focused tests in this PR.
 - If no new test is added, why not:
    - N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
 - Adds startup-doctor JSON readiness semantics for machine and human diagnostics.
 - Adds runtime-state fields for channel startup attempts, started, failed, timed out, and readiness phase.
 - http-ready is now explicitly distinct from full readiness.
 - Failed channel startup is reported as failed instead of being misclassified as started.
 - Runtime-state error/warning strings are redacted before

### Diagram (if applicable)
```bash
Before:
[gateway starts] -> [HTTP may be up] -> [channel/sidecar work unclear] -> [startup-doctor can overstate readiness]

After:
[gateway starts]
  -> [startupPhase=http-ready, readiness.fullyReady=false]
  -> [channels tracked: started/failed/timed_out]
  -> [sidecars-ready/ready, readiness.fullyReady=true when complete]
```

## Security Impact (required)
 - New permissions/capabilities? (No)
 - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes)
 - New/changed network calls? (No)
 - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes)
 - Data access scope changed? (No)
 - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation:
    - Secrets/tokens handling changed: runtime-state strings are now redacted before persistence to reduce risk of secret leakage through startup errors/warnings.
    - Command/tool execution surface changed: startup-doctor JSON behavior was added/updated for diagnostics. It reads runtime-state/local probes and does not read openclaw.json.
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
 - OS: Windows
 - Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm development checkout
 - Model/provider: N/A
 - Integration/channel (if any): gateway channel startup tests use focused fixtures/stubs
 - Relevant config (redacted): no runtime config or openclaw.json was read, copied, printed, or committed

### Steps
Checkout feature/pr-21b-normal-mode-startup-channel-backoff.
Run diff/scope checks.
Run focused gateway tests.
### Expected
 - Runtime-state sanitizes sensitive strings before persistence.
 - Startup-doctor does not read openclaw.json.
 - http-ready is not reported as full readiness.
 - Immediate channel startup rejection is counted as failed.
 - Later channels continue starting after one channel fails.
 - Slow channel startup is bounded and reported as timed_out.
### Actual
 - Focused gateway tests passed.
 - Diff check passed.
 - Typecheck could not complete due Node/V8 heap/runtime crash with no actionable TypeScript diagnostics.
### Evidence

Attach at least one:

  -  [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
  -  [ ] Trace/log snippets
  -  [ ] Screenshot/recording
  -  [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Focused validation after fixes:

```bash
git diff --check: passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/startup-runtime-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts

2 test files passed
40 tests passed
```

Typecheck note:
```bash
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json

Normal and 8GB heap attempts crashed due to Node/V8 heap/runtime failure.
No actionable TypeScript diagnostics were emitted.
```
## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

 - Verified scenarios:
    - runtime-state redaction behavior
    - redaction idempotency
    - channel startup success summary
    - immediate channel startup rejection summary
    - channel timeout summary
    - startup-doctor no longer reads source config / openclaw.json
explicit readiness object is present
 - Edge cases checked:
    - already-redacted values
    - punctuation/bracket endings
    - bearer/JWT-like/token/password/API key/credentialed URL style strings
    - one channel failing while later channel still starts
 - What you did not verify:
    - live third-party channel startup against real external services
    - full typecheck, because both normal and 8GB heap runs crashed with Node/V8 heap/runtime failure
## Review Conversations
  - I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

### Compatibility / Migration
 - Backward compatible? (Yes)
 - Config/env changes? (No)
 - Migration needed? (No)
 - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A
### Risks and Mitigations
 - Risk: timed-out channel startup tasks may continue in the background if they cannot be cancelled.
  - Mitigation: timeout state is explicitly reported via channel results/counters and documented.
 - Risk: consumers may confuse HTTP availability with full gateway readiness.
  - Mitigation: startup-doctor now exposes readiness.fullyReady; docs tell consumers not to rely on httpHealthy alone.
 - Risk: runtime-state errors could accidentally include sensitive data.
  - Mitigation: runtime-state strings are redacted before truncation and covered by focused tests.
 - Risk: full repo typecheck could not be completed in this environment due Node/V8 heap/runtime crash.
    - Mitigation: focused tests and diff checks passed; typecheck crash is documented as tooling/runtime blocked with no surfaced TypeScript diagnostics.


A small note: under **Scope**, I checked **API / contracts** because startup-doctor JSON/runtime-state shape is effectively a diagnostic contract, and **UI / DX** because this improves CLI/operator diagnostics.

target.title: feat(telegram): add progress assistant preview lane
target.body:
## Summary
- add `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` to enable assistant partial previews while staying in `mode: "progress"`
- keep the existing Telegram progress/tool/status draft on the answer lane and stream assistant partial text on a separate transient assistant lane
- clear/reset the assistant preview lane on final answer delivery and new assistant-message boundaries
- regenerate bundled channel config metadata

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram `mode: "progress"` already shows the useful live tool/status/progress draft, but it drops assistant/Nexus-style answer partials. Switching to `block`/`partial` is not acceptable because it replaces the existing progress-live behavior. This patch adds an opt-in second assistant preview lane for answer partials while leaving progress mode intact.
- **Real environment tested:** Mac mini production OpenClaw setup, Telegram enabled/OK, current installed Gateway left untouched at 2026.5.7. Worktree tested at `/Users/thomas/.openclaw/workspace/projects/openclaw-upstream/openclaw-telegram-dual-live` on branch `feat/telegram-progress-assistant-lane`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
$ openclaw status --deep | sed -n '1,80p'
OpenClaw status
...
Gateway              local · ws://127.0.0.1:18789 ... reachable 56ms ... app 2026.5.7
...
Channels
│ Telegram │ ON      │ OK     │ token config+tokenFile ... accounts 2/2 │
```

```text
$ node --import tsx - <<'NODE'
import { resolveChannelPreviewStreamMode, resolveChannelProgressDraftAssistantPreview } from './src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts';
const currentProductionStreaming = { streaming: { mode: 'progress' } };
const afterPatchStreaming = { streaming: { mode: 'progress', progress: { assistantPreview: true } } };
console.log('environment=Mac mini OpenClaw worktree');
console.log('current.mode=' + resolveChannelPreviewStreamMode(currentProductionStreaming));
console.log('current.assistantPreview=' + resolveChannelProgressDraftAssistantPreview(currentProductionStreaming));
console.log('after.mode=' + resolveChannelPreviewStreamMode(afterPatchStreaming));
console.log('after.assistantPreview=' + resolveChannelProgressDraftAssistantPreview(afterPatchStreaming));
NODE
```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal capture from the patched OpenClaw worktree:

```text
environment=Mac mini OpenClaw worktree
current.mode=progress
current.assistantPreview=false
after.mode=progress
after.assistantPreview=true
```

Supplemental local verification:

```text
$ npx pnpm config:channels:check
$ npx pnpm vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.helpers.test.ts
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       88 passed (88)

$ npx pnpm check:changed
Process exited with code 0.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The patched config resolver keeps the existing production-style `progress` mode unchanged by default (`assistantPreview=false`) and enables the new additive assistant preview only when `streaming.progress.assistantPreview=true`. The Telegram dispatch test covers the actual lane split: progress/tool updates remain on the progress draft and assistant partials update the separate assistant lane.
- **What was not tested:** Production activation against the installed Gateway, real Telegram message delivery from the patched branch, and Gateway restart were intentionally skipped. This PR is meant to be merged/released first; local production activation should happen later via config after release.

## Testing
- `npx pnpm config:channels:check`
- `npx pnpm vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.helpers.test.ts`
- `npx pnpm check:changed`

## Notes
- Existing `off`, `partial`, `block`, and `progress` semantics stay unchanged by default.
- The new behavior is opt-in via `streaming.progress.assistantPreview: true`.

target.title: Add CLI tool plan execution
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a deterministic tool-plan execution CLI that replays planned tool calls through the existing Gateway `tools.invoke` boundary instead of creating a parallel dispatcher.

- adds `executeToolPlan` with array and `{ steps }` plan support
- adds `openclaw tools execute-plan [file]` with JSON output, session/agent targeting, approval reporting, and stop-on-block defaults
- wires the new `tools` command into lazy CLI registration and command descriptors
- documents the new CLI page and navigation entry

## Why

This gives integrations and tests a stable way to take model-selected tool steps and dispatch them through OpenClaw's native tool invocation path, preserving normal tool resolution, approval, policy, and `before_tool_call` hook behavior before any tool body runs.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw tools execute-plan` can replay a JSON tool plan through the existing Gateway `tools.invoke` boundary, including plugin-owned tools and `before_tool_call` blocking before the tool body runs.

Real environment tested: Windows 11, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw source branch `codex/execute-plan-cli` at commit `8e0ecd4275`, foreground Gateway from `openclaw gateway run` on `ws://127.0.0.1:18893`, token auth, one temporary native OpenClaw plugin loaded from `plugins.load.paths`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```powershell
node --import tsx src\entry.ts --profile pr-proof tools execute-plan .artifacts\execute-plan-plugin-proof-plan.json --url ws://127.0.0.1:18893 --token local-proof-token --json --timeout 120000
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the command above:
```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "stopped": true,
  "stopReason": "blocked_tool",
  "steps": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "action": "pr_allowed_tool",
      "status": "completed",
      "durationMs": 5990,
      "source": "plugin",
      "output": {
        "content": [
          {
            "type": "text",
            "text": "allowed tool body executed"
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "index": 1,
      "action": "pr_blocked_tool",
      "status": "blocked",
      "durationMs": 4141,
      "error": {
        "code": "forbidden",
        "message": "PR proof blocked before tool body"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Plugin-side execution log from the same run:
```text
2026-05-15T20:33:12.191Z EXECUTE pr_allowed_tool input=before-block
2026-05-15T20:33:16.354Z BLOCK pr_blocked_tool input=must-not-run
```

Observed result after fix: The new CLI executed the first plugin tool through native Gateway `tools.invoke`, then the plugin `before_tool_call` hook blocked the second planned tool with `forbidden`. The blocked tool body did not log an `EXECUTE pr_blocked_tool` line, and the third planned `pr_allowed_tool` step after the blocked call did not run, proving execution stopped at the native tool boundary.

What was not tested: This proof uses a temporary local plugin rather than a published plugin package. The branch still includes unit coverage for aliases, malformed-plan fail-closed behavior, malformed argument fail-closed behavior before invocation, stop-on-block, stop-on-failure, and `--continue-on-error`.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/tools-cli.test.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/tools.md docs/cli/index.md docs/docs.json src/tools/execute-plan.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts src/cli/tools-cli.ts src/tools/index.ts src/cli/program/register.subclis-core.ts src/cli/program/subcli-descriptors.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/tools/execute-plan.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts src/cli/tools-cli.ts src/tools/index.ts src/cli/program/register.subclis-core.ts src/cli/program/subcli-descriptors.ts`
- `node --import tsx src\entry.ts --profile pr-proof tools execute-plan .artifacts\execute-plan-session-status.json --url ws://127.0.0.1:18891 --token local-proof-token --json --timeout 30000`

target.title: chore(docker): bundle channel voice plugin deps
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: default Docker builds pruned optional bundled plugin runtime dependencies unless operators knew to set `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS`.
- Why it matters: Discord, Feishu, and Voice Call are externalized plugin packages but should be ready in the standard Docker image path.
- What changed: default Docker image builds now include bundled plugin ids `discord`, `feishu`, and `voice-call`; `scripts/docker/setup.sh` uses the same default.
- What did NOT change: operators can still pass `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS=` to build a slimmer image without optional bundled plugin deps.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Docs
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Integrations
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: default Docker setup/image-build inputs now include the bundled plugin ids `discord feishu voice-call`, while an explicitly empty `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS=` remains an opt-out.

target.title: feat(telegram): bind spawned subagents to forum topics
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an explicit `supportsAutomaticThreadBindingSpawn` channel capability so automatic thread-bound session spawn support is not overloaded onto `defaultTopLevelPlacement`.
- Wires Telegram `subagent_spawning`, `subagent_delivery_target`, and `subagent_ended` hooks so `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` for subagents can create and bind a child forum topic while keeping Telegram's normal top-level binding placement as `current`.
- Makes the spawn capability per-kind (`subagent` vs `acp`) so Telegram advertises subagent forum-topic spawn support without incorrectly exposing ACP child-topic spawn.
- Documents the new capability seam and adds Telegram/core regression coverage for schema exposure, runtime capabilities, hook registration, binding, completion routing, cleanup, and boundary invariants.

## Context

Telegram already had forum-topic primitives and session binding infrastructure, but it lacked the subagent lifecycle hook wiring and an explicit capability to say: "explicit subagent thread spawns can create child topics, while ordinary top-level binding should stay in the current conversation." Discord and Matrix naturally expose automatic thread spawns through `defaultTopLevelPlacement: "child"`; Feishu keeps `current` placement and does not advertise child-thread session spawn. Telegram now follows the narrower model: keep normal Telegram `/focus` and top-level binding behavior current, while allowing explicit `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` for subagents to create a child forum topic when the Telegram adapter can do so.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram `sessions_spawn(thread:true, mode:"session")` from a channel-bound CodeClaw triage session creates and binds a real Telegram forum topic for the spawned subagent session, so follow-up work routes to that topic instead of becoming invisible or falling back to the parent topic.
- Real environment tested: Live `codeclaw-triage` Docker container after installing a custom OpenClaw package built from combined proof branch `codeclaw-proof-82023-82275` (https://github.com/efpiva/openclaw/tree/codeclaw-proof-82023-82275) at `OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (db1c53a)`. The container was connected to the real CodeClaw Telegram forum supergroup and used the real CodeClaw triage cron job.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  docker exec --user codeclaw codeclaw-triage     HOME=/home/codeclaw openclaw cron run cc373f35-b483-4c7f-9253-2f6c9c94a595       --wait --wait-timeout 15m --timeout 30000
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  The triage transcript for run `1d6d77dd-c23b-4b02-9831-e990fb11a7fc` shows the initial PR action for private CodeClaw PR #1223 was spawned with `thread:true` and `mode:"session"`:

  ```json
  {
    "type": "toolCall",
    "name": "sessions_spawn",
    "arguments": {
      "agentId": "main",
      "label": "codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223",
      "mode": "session",
      "context": "isolated",
      "thread": true
    }
  }
  ```

  The tool result accepted the spawn and returned a thread-bound child session:

  ```json
  {
    "toolName": "sessions_spawn",
    "isError": false,
    "details": {
      "status": "accepted",
      "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819",
      "runId": "23538111-3540-4645-a66b-cc0568f96de0",
      "mode": "session",
      "note": "thread-bound session stays active after this task; continue in-thread for follow-ups.",
      "modelApplied": true
    }
  }
  ```

  Live Telegram/OpenClaw logs show the forum topic was created and bound to that child session. The Telegram supergroup id is redacted, but the topic id and session key are unchanged:

  ```text
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.282-07:00 telegram: bound conversation <telegram-supergroup-id>:topic:1167 -> agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819 (idle=24h maxAge=disabled)
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.406-07:00 [telegram] update: {"message":{"message_id":1167,"chat":{"title":"CodeClaw Activity","is_forum":true,"type":"supergroup"},"message_thread_id":1167,"forum_topic_created":{"name":"🤖 codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223"},"is_topic_message":true}}
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.923-07:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run start: runId=23538111-3540-4645-a66b-cc0568f96de0 sessionId=ada5b4d0-65b5-48ff-8c81-76c2ab477ab8 provider=github-copilot model=gpt-5.5 thinking=medium messageChannel=telegram
  2026-05-17T15:07:58.444-07:00 [agent/embedded] [context-diag] pre-prompt: sessionKey=agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819 ... sessionFile=/home/codeclaw/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ada5b4d0-65b5-48ff-8c81-76c2ab477ab8-topic-1167.jsonl
  ```

  The persisted Telegram binding file contains the same mapping:

  ```json
  {
    "conversationId": "<telegram-supergroup-id>:topic:1167",
    "targetKind": "subagent",
    "targetSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819",
    "agentId": "main",
    "label": "codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223",
    "metadata": {
      "threadName": "🤖 codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223"
    }
  }
  ```

  The CodeClaw poller state also persisted the spawned native session key for that PR:

  ```json
  {
    "source": "pr_reviews",
    "id": "microsoft.ghe.com|bic/lobster#1223",
    "lastKind": "initial",
    "nativeSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819"
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram Bot API created forum topic `1167`, OpenClaw bound it to the spawned `main` subagent session, the child session ran with `messageChannel=telegram`, and all child/specialist session transcript files for the spawned review used the `topic-1167` suffix. CodeClaw recorded `nativeSessionKey` for future follow-ups.
- What was not tested: Telegram permission-denied and non-forum failure paths in the live container; those remain covered by local mocked tests. The live proof used one real forum supergroup and one real CodeClaw triage run.

## Supplemental validation


Behavior addressed: Telegram subagent `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` now has an explicit per-kind automatic-spawn capability and lifecycle hooks that bind spawned subagents to child forum topics without enabling Telegram ACP child-topic spawns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with mocked Telegram forum topic creation and session binding adapters; no live Telegram Bot API/forum supergroup was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/index.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.test.ts \
  src/channels/plugins/thread-binding-api.test.ts \
  src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.test.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-capabilities.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/boundary-invariants.test.ts
pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md
git diff --check
pnpm build
codex review --uncommitted
```

Evidence after fix: Targeted Vitest proof passed with 12 files and 245 tests; docs MDX check passed; whitespace check passed; `pnpm build` completed including plugin SDK export verification; final Codex CLI review reported no actionable correctness issues.

Observed result after fix: Telegram advertises `thread` for subagent session spawn through the new explicit per-kind capability, returns a routable Telegram delivery origin for created forum topics, routes completion to the bound topic, cleans up Telegram subagent bindings, and does not advertise/recommend ACP thread-bound spawns when ACP support is disabled for Telegram.

What was not tested: Live Telegram Bot API behavior in a real forum supergroup with `can_manage_topics`; non-forum group and permission failure behavior were covered only through local unit paths/mocked failures.

target.title: feat(tools): add text-only profile
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a text-only tool profile that explicitly denies all tools.
- Surfaces text-only in tool catalog/default profile metadata.
- Allows managed screenwatch frames in local media roots.
- Updates plugin trust audit coverage so text-only is treated as restrictive.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Text-only tool profile should resolve to a no-tool policy and should not make allowlisted extension plugin tools reachable under the plugin trust audit.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.5, Node v24.14.0, production TypeScript imports via tsx, temporary OPENCLAW_HOME and OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR with a real installed extension directory at state/extensions/some-plugin.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ tmp=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$tmp/state/extensions/some-plugin" "$tmp/home"
$ OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmp/home" HOME="$tmp/home" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmp/state" node --import tsx -e 'import { collectPluginsTrustFindings } from "./src/security/audit-plugins-trust.ts"; import { resolveCoreToolProfilePolicy } from "./src/agents/tool-catalog.ts"; const cfg = { plugins: { allow: ["some-plugin"] }, tools: { profile: "text-only" } }; const findings = await collectPluginsTrustFindings({ cfg, stateDir: process.env.OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ textOnlyPolicy: resolveCoreToolProfilePolicy("text-only"), pluginReachabilityFindings: findings.filter((finding) => finding.checkId === "plugins.tools_reachable_permissive_policy").length, allFindingIds: findings.map((finding) => finding.checkId) }, null, 2));'
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
{
  "textOnlyPolicy": {
    "deny": [
      "*"
    ]
  },
  "pluginReachabilityFindings": 0,
  "allFindingIds": []
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real runtime profile resolver returns `deny: ["*"]` for text-only, and the production plugin trust audit returns zero `plugins.tools_reachable_permissive_policy` findings for an allowlisted installed extension under that profile.
- What was not tested: None

## Test Plan
- git diff origin/main...HEAD --check
- targeted secret scan over PR diff
- pnpm exec vitest run src/security/audit-plugins-trust.test.ts src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.test.ts --reporter=verbose
- pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.media.config.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts --reporter=verbose
- pnpm check

target.title: fix(webchat): preserve code block whitespace
target.body:
Fixes #81339

## Summary
- stop trimming normal WebChat send payloads before `chat.send`
- keep slash/local command matching, dedup, and history on the trimmed text while preserving the user-authored chat text on the wire
- preserve queued and steered queued message whitespace for code blocks and ASCII diagrams

## Real behavior proof

Re-implemented and re-proven on current `main` (rebased to head `6da9e274c2`; the prior head was ~690 commits behind and `main` has refactored the Control UI composer pipeline since the original proof).

**Behavior addressed:** WebChat must send fenced code blocks / ASCII diagrams exactly as typed, so leading spaces and trailing newlines survive instead of being stripped by `.trim()` before `chat.send`.

**Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw gateway built from this PR head on macOS. Control UI rebuilt with `pnpm ui:build`; isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`/`OPENCLAW_HOME`; `gateway run --auth token` on `127.0.0.1:18897` with `gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true` so headless Chrome could connect over local HTTP. The served `/chat` Control UI was driven with Playwright (system Chrome, headless) and the outgoing `chat.send` WebSocket frame was captured via CDP.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. `pnpm ui:build`
2. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$H OPENCLAW_HOME=$H pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18897 --token <tok> --auth token --force --bind loopback`
3. Playwright: open `http://127.0.0.1:18897/chat#token=<tok>`, type `` ```\n    indented one\n        indented two\n```\n `` (with extra leading spaces and a trailing newline) into the composer, send, capture the `framesent` WebSocket payload whose method is `chat.send`.

**Evidence after fix:** captured `chat.send` frame (verbatim, token/ids redacted):
```json
{"type":"req","method":"chat.send","params":{"sessionKey":"agent:main:main","message":"```\n    indented one\n        indented two\n```\n","deliver":false}}
```
Programmatic assertions on the captured frame: `sentMessageEqualsRaw: true`, `keepsLeadingSpaces: true`, `keepsTrailingNewline: true`, `pageErrors: []`.

**Observed result after fix:** the served Control UI sends the user text byte-for-byte; the 4-space and 8-space code-block indentation and the trailing newline are preserved on the wire. The whitespace-preservation contract is also locked by a regression test asserting the emitted `chat.send` payload keeps leading/trailing whitespace: `ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` -> "preserves user-authored whitespace (code blocks / ASCII) in chat.send" (85/85 `app-chat` tests pass; `oxfmt --check` clean; `git diff --check` clean).

**What was not tested:** end-to-end model response rendering (no live model was configured for the proof gateway; the proof targets the client send path, which is where this fix lives). Local browser run used `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth` only to bypass the pairing ceremony over local HTTP; production device auth is unchanged.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve persistent rich message line breaks
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves Fixes #95409. Telegram persistent rich messages can collapse ordinary final-answer line breaks when `richMessages` is enabled. The plain `sendMessage` path preserves a normal assistant reply like: ```text 第一行 第二行 第三行 ``` but the Bot API 10.1 persistent rich-message path sends `rich_message.html`, where literal text-node `\n` is not a reliable visual line break. This makes structured replies look compressed even though the account explicitly opted into rich messages. This PR fixes the supported persistent rich-message path only. It does **not** use `sendRichMessageDraft`, does not change native draft streaming behavior, and does not introduce an extra final message bubble. ## Summary - Preserve text-node line breaks during Telegram rich HTML preparation. - Convert ordinary non-whitespace text-node `\n` to `
` before sending `rich_message.html`. - Leave pure structural whitespace between tags unchanged. - Leave newlines inside `
` / `` literal.
- Add regression tests for rich final-message line breaks and preformatted/code blocks.

## Implementation Notes

The previous preparation path only sanitized rich HTML and enforced Telegram's nesting limit:

```ts
function prepareTelegramRichHtml(html: string): string {
  return limitTelegramRichHtmlNesting(sanitizeTelegramRichHtml(html), TELEGRAM_RICH_NESTING_LIMIT);
}
```

The new helper runs after sanitization and before nesting-limit enforcement. It walks HTML tags, tracks `
` / `` depth, and only materializes newlines in non-whitespace text nodes. That keeps existing HTML structure and code/preformatted content stable while making normal assistant text readable in Telegram Rich Messages.

## Evidence

Source-level reproduction on latest `openclaw/openclaw` main checked at `955f3ed09465f0a475776335c2fe205ef84d6a85`:

- `extensions/telegram/src/rich-message.ts` prepared `rich_message.html` with sanitize + nesting limit only.
- No existing code path materialized ordinary text-node newlines as `
` for persistent rich final messages.
- The affected path is `sendRichMessage` / `editMessageText({ rich_message })`, not `sendRichMessageDraft`.

Local lightweight validation after rebasing onto latest `upstream/main` (`955f3ed09465f0a475776335c2fe205ef84d6a85`):

```text
git diff --check
# passed

git diff -- extensions/telegram/src | grep -q 'sendRichMessageDraft'
# no sendRichMessageDraft changes
```

Regression coverage added:

- `preserves plain line breaks in native rich final messages`
- `keeps preformatted rich HTML newlines literal`

Real Telegram behavior observed in a direct chat with `richMessages` enabled:

- ordinary final reply line breaks were readable through the plain `sendMessage` path;
- the persistent rich-message path could visually compress ordinary line-separated text;
- a candidate patch restored the expected line breaks while still using `sendRichMessage` with `rich_message.html`;
- inspected outbound payload shape used `sendRichMessage` and had ordinary text line breaks materialized as `
`.

I did not run the full local test suite in this worktree because dependencies were not installed locally; GitHub Actions should be treated as the authoritative validation gate for this OpenClaw PR.

## Risk / Rollback

Risk is limited to Telegram accounts that opted into rich messages. The change is conservative because it only affects text nodes that contain non-whitespace content and avoids preformatted/code blocks.

Rollback: revert this PR to return rich HTML preparation to sanitize + nesting limit only.

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

TL;DR:
Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; goal: achieve parity for Claude

New feature; issue https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93793

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK capabilities and features.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

Harness: this PR
JSON-RPC server: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio, directly wrapping the latest claude agent SDK. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge`  https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server -- https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix (fresh capture, 2026-05-28):** This gateway runs the claude-bridge harness as the default runtime for all Anthropic models (`agents.defaults.models.*.agentRuntime.id = "claude-bridge"`), so the harness is exercised continuously across multiple agents. Captured live:
  1. **Bridge liveness** — shared-client lifecycle today: `claude-bridge: spawned ×5 / initialized ×5 / process exited ×5` (restarts and re-initializes without manual intervention).
  2. **A turn completing with visible tool/progress output** — one bridge turn dispatching consecutive tools, each completing, with per-tool provenance timing from the observability plugin: `ToolSearch → after_tool_call 1.9ms`, `exec → 1.7ms`, `Write → 1.9ms`, `Agent → 1.9ms`.
  3. **Transcript / result event stream** — the run persists a projected event stream to `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`: parentId-chained `message` events covering the user turn, assistant `tool_use` blocks, and `tool_result` blocks.
  4. **`/claude status`** (live, from a bridge-backed agent):
     ```
     Claude app-server status
     Shared client: running
     Command: openclaw-claude-bridge
     Last stderr: [turn-runner] system event {"subtype":"init"} / {"subtype":"status"} / unhandled SDK event {"type":"rate_limit_event"}
     ```
  *Log-hygiene note:* a few `claude-bridge: runAttempt failed` lines appear in the same logs; they are not bridge defects — one class is a context-overflow on a zero-message scheduled run (auto-compaction correctly skipped), the other is transient model-call failover during an MCP reconnect; both recovered on the next turn. Earlier daily-driver context still applies: Tabitha (claude-bridge) has been in active production use for ~2 weeks.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- Real world use for over a month on a broad diversity of tasks with observed, anecdotal, improvements in coding, token usage and well executed long running tasks
- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.

target.title: feat(senseaudio): add SenseAudio TTS provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `senseaudio` bundled plugin only has ASR; no TTS.
- Why it matters: completes the round trip in the same plugin; SenseAudio's `/v1/t2a_v2` is solid TTS and pairs with the ASR added in #66943.
- What changed: registers a `speechProvider` in `extensions/senseaudio/`. Synthesizes via `/v1/t2a_v2`, lists system voices via `/v1/get_voice`, transcodes MP3→opus for `voice-note` targets. Errors split into HTTP / `base_resp.status_code` / missing-audio buckets.
- What did NOT change: no core code; ASR untouched; streaming, telephony, voice cloning, text-generated voices out of scope.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related # 66943
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed:** SenseAudio bundled plugin now registers a `speechProvider` alongside the existing ASR provider. With `SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY` set, `isConfigured` returns true, `listVoices` surfaces the upstream `system_voice` set via `POST /v1/get_voice`, and `synthesize` returns a real MP3 (or transcoded opus on `voice-note` targets).
- **Real environment tested:** Yes (Linux 5.14.0, Node 22.22.3, production `https://api.senseaudio.cn` with a valid `SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY`, commit `cf9d686a66`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  export SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY=...   # redacted
  pnpm openclaw infer tts convert \
    --text "Hello, OpenClaw, this is senseaudio TTS provider" \
    --model senseaudio/senseaudio-tts-1.5-260319 \
    --voice female_0033_b \
    --output ./hello.mp3 \
    --local --json
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** Captured live stdout from running the `openclaw` command above on head commit `cf9d686a66`:

  ```json
  {
    "ok": true,
    "capability": "tts.convert",
    "transport": "local",
    "provider": "senseaudio",
    "attempts": [
      {
        "provider": "senseaudio",
        "outcome": "success",
        "reasonCode": "success",
        "personaBinding": "none",
        "latencyMs": 3595
      }
    ],
    "outputs": [
      {
        "path": "/mnt/cache/haoxin/vc/openclaw/hello.mp3",
        "format": "mp3",
        "voiceCompatible": false
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

  ![terminal screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d62bae66-0e43-4d3e-b506-b78646934335)

  The produced `./hello.mp3` is a valid MP3 file (ID3 header) that plays back as the input text. Attached audio artifact: [hello.mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27789675/hello.mp3).

- **Observed result after fix:** SenseAudio TTS provider registered and reachable end-to-end via the gateway-bypassing `--local` path; voice-list endpoint reachable; produced MP3 plays back as the input text.

- **What was not tested:**
  - Voice-note opus transcoding end-to-end through a real messaging channel (Telegram / iMessage / WhatsApp).
  - Telephony, streaming, voice cloning, and text-generated voices (intentionally out of scope).
  - Full `pnpm test` / `pnpm test:contracts:channels` suites — only targeted `pnpm test:extension senseaudio` and `pnpm test:contracts:plugins` lanes were executed.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A (feature, not a bug fix).
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — new feature, no regression surface.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/senseaudio/tts.test.ts`, `extensions/senseaudio/speech-provider.test.ts`, `extensions/senseaudio/senseaudio.live.test.ts`, and `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-registration.senseaudio.contract.test.ts` (driven by the new entry in `src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-registration-contract-cases.ts`).
- Scenario the test should lock in: registration contract guards the `speechProvider` id and `listVoices` requirement; network-layer tests lock in the `/v1/t2a_v2` body shape, the three error buckets, the hex decode, and the `/v1/get_voice` "only system voices" mapping; adapter-layer tests lock in config / env-var fallback / `isConfigured` / minimal `parseDirectiveToken` / both `synthesize` paths; live test guards the production round trip.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: those are the only surfaces refactors can plausibly break — upstream contract drift, talk-voice wiring, registered-capability loss.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A — tests added by this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: tests are included in this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `senseaudio` appears in `openclaw infer tts providers`, "configured" when `SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY` is in env or `messages.tts.providers.senseaudio.apiKey`.
- `openclaw infer tts voices --provider senseaudio` lists the upstream system voices.
- `openclaw infer tts convert --model senseaudio/senseaudio-tts-1.5-260319 ...` returns MP3 (or opus for voice-note channel targets).
- `autoSelectOrder: 45` — strictly behind ElevenLabs (20), Azure (30), MiniMax (40). Existing user defaults are not displaced.
- Defaults: voice `female_0033_b`, model `senseaudio-tts-1.5-260319`, base URL `https://api.senseaudio.cn`, MP3 32 kHz / 128 kbps / stereo.
- No new env vars / config keys / CLI flags. `SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY` was already declared by the ASR side.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
audio-file target:
  tts.convert -> senseaudio.synthesize
              -> POST /v1/t2a_v2 -> hex audio
              -> Buffer.from(hex) -> MP3 (voiceCompatible=false)

voice-note target:
  tts.convert (channel=voice-note) -> senseaudio.synthesize
              -> POST /v1/t2a_v2 -> MP3
              -> transcodeAudioBufferToOpus (ffmpeg)
              -> opus (voiceCompatible=true) -> messenger voice-note bubble

voice picker:
  talk UI -> senseaudio.listVoices
          -> POST /v1/get_voice {voice_type: "system"}
          -> map system_voice[] -> SpeechVoiceOption[]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` (reuses `SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY`; configured values go through `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString`; key never logged).
- New/changed network calls? `Yes`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Two new outbound HTTPS calls to `api.senseaudio.cn` (`/v1/t2a_v2`, `/v1/get_voice`). Both routed through `fetchWithSsrFGuard` + `ssrfPolicyFromHttpBaseUrlAllowedHostname`. HTTP errors via `assertOkOrThrowProviderError`. Business errors surface `status_msg`, never the request key. `voice-note` transcoding reuses the SDK-managed `transcodeAudioBufferToOpus`. `listVoices` enforces a 15s timeout.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (5.14.0-284.25.1.el9_2.x86_64)
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.3 (nvm); pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: SenseAudio `senseaudio-tts-1.5-260319`
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A for the CLI repro
- Relevant config (redacted): `SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY=sk-...` in env; no config-file changes required

### Steps

1. `export SENSEAUDIO_API_KEY=sk-...`
2. `pnpm openclaw infer tts convert --text "Hello, OpenClaw" --model senseaudio/senseaudio-tts-1.5-260319 --voice female_0033_b --output ./hello.mp3 --local --json`
3. Inspect the JSON envelope and play `./hello.mp3`.

### Expected

- Envelope: `provider: "senseaudio"`, `outputs[0].format: "mp3"`, writable `path`.
- `./hello.mp3` is a valid MP3 that plays back the input text.

### Actual

- Envelope exactly as expected.
- `./hello.mp3` plays back as the input.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: live `tts convert` returns a playable MP3; `pnpm test:extension senseaudio` 31/31; three boundary inventories 0 violations; tsgo/oxlint/oxfmt clean; senseaudio plugin-registration contract passes.
- Edge cases checked: `base_resp.status_code != 0` surfaces `status_msg`; missing `data.audio` triggers "no audio data"; `listVoices` drops cloning/generation, only maps `system_voice`; empty `description: []` doesn't leak as empty string; `voice-note` path invokes the transcoder and flips `voiceCompatible: true`; API key resolution falls back from config to env to "missing".
- What you did **not** verify: voice-note opus end-to-end through a real messenger; telephony / streaming / voice cloning / text-generated voices (all intentionally out of scope).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: existing users with another default TTS provider might be silently reassigned to SenseAudio.
  - Mitigation: `autoSelectOrder: 45` — strictly behind every existing TTS provider; mirrors the ASR side's `autoPriority: 40`.
- Risk: voice-note opus transcoding depends on ffmpeg.
  - Mitigation: reuses the same `transcodeAudioBufferToOpus` SDK helper that MiniMax already relies on; no new ffmpeg dependency or failure semantics.
- Risk: a slow upstream could hang the voice-picker dropdown.
  - Mitigation: `listSenseAudioSystemVoices` carries a 15s `SENSEAUDIO_LIST_VOICES_TIMEOUT_MS`.
- Risk: a hostile upstream returning a huge hex payload could allocate a large `Buffer`.
  - Mitigation: SSRF guard restricts the host; can add a hard byte cap in a follow-up if needed.

## AI-assisted

Developed with **Claude Code**. Implementation, tests, and review iterations generated and applied by Claude; the contributor reviewed each step, supplied credentials, and ran the live verification locally.
