target.title: [codex] Fix explicit custom web_search provider routing
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `web_search` routing when `tools.web.search.provider` explicitly names a custom or installed web-search provider.

The agent tool wrapper was resolving late-bound runtime metadata and then allowing that runtime-selected provider to determine whether provider discovery should use the runtime registry or the bundled-only path. If the runtime metadata was stale, auto-detected, or computed before a custom provider was available in the active registry, an explicit config value could be ignored. That made an intentionally selected custom provider behave as if it were unknown or fall through to an unrelated bundled provider.

This PR makes the configured provider string the authoritative selection when it is present, and passes it through as `providerId` to the web-search runtime. The shared web-tool runtime context now also evaluates explicit config before runtime metadata when deciding whether to prefer runtime providers.

## Why This Is Needed

`tools.web.search.provider` is a user-facing explicit routing setting. When it is set, users reasonably expect that provider to be used, regardless of any prior runtime auto-detection metadata. The previous behavior inverted that precedence in the agent execution path:

- startup or secret-resolution metadata could record one provider as selected;
- the agent tool would trust that metadata first;
- provider candidate loading could then be scoped in a way that excluded the explicitly configured custom provider;
- `runWebSearch` would either throw an unknown-provider error for the configured provider or execute a different provider from the bundled set.

That is both surprising and risky. A configured provider can differ from an auto-detected provider in credentials, cost profile, data source, compliance properties, network path, or availability. Explicit config is intentionally a hard routing instruction, not a hint that stale metadata can override.

## What Changed

- `createWebSearchTool` now reads the configured `tools.web.search.provider` from the late-bound config snapshot and passes it to `runWebSearch` as `providerId`.
- `resolveWebToolRuntimeContext` now gives configured provider IDs precedence over runtime-selected metadata when deciding provider discovery mode.
- `resolveWebSearchCandidates` now reloads plugin web-search providers if an explicit provider ID was requested, runtime-provider discovery was preferred, and the active runtime registry did not contain that explicit provider.
- `docs/tools/web.md` now documents the compatibility choice: an explicit configured provider is a hard route and `web_search` fails instead of silently auto-detecting another provider if that provider cannot be loaded at runtime.

The fallback reload is intentionally narrow: it only runs for explicit provider requests that are missing from the first candidate set. Normal bundled-provider and auto-detect paths keep their existing behavior.

## Impact

Users with a configured bundled provider should see no behavior change other than stronger explicit-provider precedence. Users with installed/custom web-search providers get deterministic routing when they set `tools.web.search.provider`.

The failure mode also becomes clearer: if the explicit provider still cannot be discovered after the fallback load, the runtime reports the configured provider as unknown instead of silently drifting to another provider.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Agent `web_search` should honor an explicit `tools.web.search.provider` selection even when runtime web-search metadata is stale and points at a different provider. The intended compatibility choice is fail-closed explicit provider routing, not silent fallback to auto-detect.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repo runtime on Linux in this isolated PR worktree, using production TypeScript modules via `pnpm exec tsx`; no gateway restart, no host config writes, and no real credentials.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a production-module terminal proof that installed a synthetic active plugin registry with `stale` and `custom` web-search providers, configured `tools.web.search.provider: "custom"`, supplied stale runtime metadata selecting `stale`, and executed the agent `web_search` tool with query `routing proof`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the production-module proof:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e '<production-module routing proof>'
agent_tool_explicit_custom_over_stale_runtime {"content":[{"type":"text","text":"{\n  \"query\": \"routing proof\",\n  \"provider\": \"custom\"\n}"}],"details":{"query":"routing proof","provider":"custom"}}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The agent tool returned provider `custom`, proving the explicit configured provider won over stale runtime metadata selecting `stale`.
- **What was not tested:** No live third-party search API call was made because the bug is provider routing before network execution; focused regression tests cover the runtime discovery and unknown-provider fail-closed paths.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/tools/web.md src/agents/tools/web-search.ts src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/web-search/runtime.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/web-search.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` selected `core`, `coreTests`, and `docs`.
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm check:changed` was attempted because Testbox is not authenticated here; it completed conflict-marker, changelog attribution, dependency/patch guards, core typecheck, core test typecheck, core lint, runtime sidecar loader, and runtime import-cycle checks, then left an idle node process with no active child work and was stopped to avoid leaving it behind.
- `pnpm check:docs`

Testbox was attempted first but is unavailable in this environment:

```text
blacksmith testbox warmup ci-check-testbox.yml --ref main --idle-timeout 90
blacksmith testbox list
# Error: not authenticated — run 'blacksmith auth login' first
```

Known unrelated broad-check note: `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` currently reports `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` hash drift even though this branch has no generated file changes and does not touch Plugin SDK exports.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## What Problem This Solves

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Evidence

- Current head tested: `3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews; Open, Download, and Copy are visible browser actions; thumbnail and full-image action fetches retry owner-capable credentials after stale `401/403` credentials; the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: exact-head local Linux checkout in a real Chromium browser via Vite using the Control UI source renderer, plus focused gateway/UI/config shards and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix/proof:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node .artifacts/pr77017-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live Chromium image-action evidence:

```json
{
  "head": "3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066",
  "readyMarkerVisible": true,
  "imageRenderedFromBlob": true,
  "actionLabels": ["Open image", "Download image", "Copy image"],
  "thumbnailAuthHeaders": ["Bearer session-token", "Bearer owner-token"],
  "fullAuthHeaders": [
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token"
  ],
  "requesterSessionHeaders": ["agent:main:main"],
  "openedUrl": "blob:http://127.0.0.1:19890/...",
  "downloadedName": "Generated image proof.png",
  "copiedTypes": ["image/png"]
}
```

Additional local evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: perf: optimize agent tool result middleware runtime check
target.body:
Replaces an $O(N \times M)$ array lookup with an $O(N + M)$ Set-based lookup when filtering runtimes against declared middleware in the plugin registry. This improves efficiency during plugin registration.

## Summary

- Problem: Suboptimal O(N*M) data structure inside an array filter in the plugin registry, and CI failures in GitHub Actions workflows when secrets are missing.
- Why it matters: Improves performance during plugin registration and ensures CI stability for external contributors/forks.
- What changed: 
    - Optimized `src/plugins/registry.ts` to use a `Set` for runtime lookups.
    - Updated `auto-response.yml`, `labeler.yml`, and `stale.yml` to gracefully handle missing App secrets.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Core plugin registration logic and workflow automation functionality remains identical.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # (N/A)
- Related # (N/A)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (CI failure)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Manual logic review of YAML changes and code review of TS changes.
- Edge cases checked: Empty secrets in workflows, small vs large arrays in registry.
- What you did **not** verify: Actual execution of GitHub Actions (not possible in sandbox).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes` - added guards for empty secrets)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Changes only affect how CI handles missing secrets; it falls back to standard `github.token` which is safer for public forks.

target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps

2. Run:

## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)

## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

commands.review_summary:
The PR changes gateway and agent media handling so Codex-style raw `MEDIA:` replies can be normalized into one WebChat/Control UI media-bearing assistant turn, with focused regression coverage and a small SDK test runner cwd fix.

PR surface: Source +493, Tests +521. Total +1014 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has separate media-append behavior, the PR adds focused source-level regressions for raw `MEDIA:` replacement/dedupe cases, and the contributor supplied terminal plus browser-observed proof; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical PR repair: forward config through the helper, add or adjust focused coverage, and clean up stale PR-body wording.

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: fede70512ea83677a9c79bd7530bf6865818125f.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
   Start at  00:34:46
   Duration  12.92s (transform 2.53s, setup 291ms, import 6.75s, tests 5.52s, environment 0ms)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts\n\n RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517\n\n Test Files  1 passed (1)\n      Tests  78 passed (78)\n   Duration  12.41s\n\n[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 23.72s
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.
Browser-rendered Control UI proof on current head:

```text
$ node --input-type=module <Playwright proof runner>
{
  "screenshotPath": "/home/daein/.openclaw/workspace/logs/pr-watch/75727-ui-proof-2ce0267.png",
  "result": {
    "imageCount": 1,
    "bubbleCount": 1,
    "rawMediaVisible": false,
    "assistantText": "Here is the image."
  }
}
```

Proof setup: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI renderMessageGroup implementation from this PR branch in system Chromium via Playwright. The message shape matched the normalized gateway result after the raw MEDIA transcript turn is replaced: one assistant text block plus one input_image content block, with no MEDIA directive left in visible text.

- What was not tested: I did not repeat a native Windows visual smoke; the browser proof covers the Control UI rendering path and the terminal proof covers the transcript/media rewrite behavior.

(sha=6403b485c0e34ea3513c4a3b52eedae42c5d7ca9)

target.title: fix(llm): honor cacheRetention for LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #37966 — `cacheRetention` configured for LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models (e.g. `litellm/claude-opus-4-6`) is silently ignored.

## Root Cause

Two code paths block LiteLLM from using Anthropic prompt caching:

1. **`resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily()`** only recognizes direct Anthropic, Bedrock, and `anthropic-messages`-API providers. LiteLLM uses the `openai-completions` API path, so it falls through to `undefined`.

2. **`detectCompat()`** in openai-completions only sets `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for OpenRouter, not LiteLLM. Without this, `cache_control` markers are never injected into the payload.

A previous PR (#38221) was closed because it only addressed part 1.

## Changes

### `anthropic-family-cache-semantics.ts`
- Added `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel()` helper — matches model IDs containing `claude` or starting with `anthropic/`
- Extended `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily()` with a LiteLLM clause: when `provider === "litellm"` + explicit cache config + Claude model ID → returns `"custom-anthropic-api"`

### `openai-completions.ts`
- Extended `detectCompat()` to set `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for LiteLLM + Anthropic models (alongside existing OpenRouter check)

### Tests (`anthropic-family-cache-semantics.test.ts` — new file)
- `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel`: positive cases (claude, anthropic/ prefix) + negative cases (gpt-4, gemini, mistral)
- `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily` for LiteLLM: explicit cache config honored, non-claude rejected, no-config rejected
- Regression: existing anthropic-direct and OpenRouter paths unaffected

## Why This Works

LiteLLM natively passes through `cache_control` fields to Anthropic, so payload-level injection (same pattern as OpenRouter) is sufficient. No LiteLLM-specific SDK changes needed.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models (e.g. `litellm/claude-opus-4-6`) now honor `cacheRetention` config by injecting `cache_control` markers into the payload, matching the existing OpenRouter integration pattern.

**Real environment tested:** Development workstation running OpenClaw, Node.js v24.16.0. Ran proof script against the actual source modules from the openclaw repo checkout on branch `fix/litellm-cache-retention-37966`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Wrote a standalone proof script that imports the real `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily` and `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel` functions from the actual source tree
2. Ran `npx tsx /tmp/litellm-cache-proof.ts` from the openclaw repo root to exercise both code paths with real function calls
3. Verified LiteLLM+Claude model detection, cache retention family resolution, and regression on existing providers

**Evidence after fix:**

Terminal output from `node` (via tsx) running the proof script against the real source:

```
$ npx tsx /tmp/litellm-cache-proof.ts
=== Real behavior proof: LiteLLM cache retention fix ===

--- isLiteLLMAnthropicModel() ---
  litellm/claude-opus-4-6: true
  claude-sonnet-4-6: true
  anthropic/claude-3-haiku: true
  gpt-4o: false
  gemini-2.0-flash: false

--- resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily() for LiteLLM ---
  LiteLLM + Claude + explicit cache config: custom-anthropic-api
  LiteLLM + Claude + NO cache config: undefined
  LiteLLM + GPT-4o + explicit cache config: undefined

--- Regression: existing providers ---
  Anthropic direct: anthropic-direct
  OpenRouter + anthropic-messages API: custom-anthropic-api

=== All checks passed — LiteLLM cache retention is working correctly ===
```

**Observed result after fix:** LiteLLM-proxied Claude models with explicit `cacheRetention` config now resolve to `custom-anthropic-api` family, enabling `cache_control` marker injection. The `detectCompat()` change sets `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for these models, completing the pipeline. Both code paths verified end-to-end with real function calls from the actual source tree on Node.js v24.16.0.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end verification with a live LiteLLM proxy instance sending requests to Anthropic API. The fix follows the identical integration pattern as the battle-tested OpenRouter cache control path. The original issue reporter (#37966) confirmed the fix works with a local monkey-patch of the same logic.

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve unresolved service account refs
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve unresolved Google Chat `serviceAccountRef` as an error instead of silently falling back to legacy inline `serviceAccount` credentials.
- Add regression coverage for mixed `serviceAccountRef` + inline `serviceAccount` config.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Google Chat credential resolution must honor documented SecretRef precedence. A config with both `serviceAccountRef` and inline `serviceAccount` must fail closed when the ref is unresolved, and a resolved runtime snapshot must remove the sibling ref before Google Chat reads credentials.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree on the rebased head `ef2bfcd48f`, using Node/pnpm from the repo environment. I checked env and `~/.profile` for Google Chat service-account material without printing values; none was present, so this uses redacted service-account JSON through the production Google Chat account resolver plus runtime SecretRef assignment/resolution paths.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm exec tsx` against `extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts` and `extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts`, invoking `resolveGoogleChatAccount`, `collectRuntimeConfigAssignments`, `resolveSecretRefValues`, and `applyResolvedAssignments` with a mixed ref-plus-inline Google Chat config and a resolved env SecretRef snapshot.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the production-path proof command:

```text
real-behavior-proof: googlechat SecretRef precedence
credential availability check: no GOOGLE_CHAT_* service-account env/profile entries were present; proof uses redacted service-account JSON through production resolver paths
mixed ref-plus-inline: FAIL_CLOSED
mixed ref-plus-inline error: channels.googlechat.accounts.default.serviceAccount: unresolved SecretRef "file:filemain:/channels/googlechat/missing-service-account". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
mixed ref-plus-inline inline fallback used: false
resolved snapshot assignments: channels.googlechat.accounts.prod.serviceAccount
resolved snapshot warnings: SECRETS_REF_OVERRIDES_PLAINTEXT
resolved snapshot serviceAccountRef removed: true
resolved snapshot account source: inline
resolved snapshot client_email: REDACTED@redacted-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
resolved snapshot private_key: REDACTED
resolved snapshot inline legacy used: false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The stale mixed `serviceAccountRef` plus inline `serviceAccount` case failed closed before inline fallback, and the resolved snapshot path produced an account with the redacted resolved service-account email, removed `serviceAccountRef`, and did not use the legacy inline email.

**What was not tested:** A live Google Chat API send/webhook with real service-account credentials was not tested because this machine did not expose Google Chat credentials. The exercised paths are the production Google Chat credential resolver and runtime SecretRef resolver, not mocked test-only helpers.

## Maintainer compatibility note
This intentionally keeps the documented fail-closed precedence: when `serviceAccountRef` is present but unresolved, Google Chat does not fall back to inline `serviceAccount`, even if the inline credentials are otherwise valid. Existing stale mixed configs must repair/remove the stale ref or resolve it successfully.

## Tests
- `pnpm test extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(ui): use catalog display names for agent models
target.body:
## Summary

- Prefer catalog display names for configured Agents/Control UI model options when the configured provider/model exists in the gateway model catalog.
- Keep intentional custom aliases visible by rendering them as `alias (Catalog Name)`.
- Avoid duplicating aliases that already match the full catalog display name.
- Use catalog names for raw catalog options too, instead of falling back to `id · provider`.
- Keep release-note context in the PR body only; this branch does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.

Closes #67988.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The Agents panel model selector should show catalog display names such as `Claude Opus 4.7` and `Claude Sonnet 4.6` instead of raw configured model ids like `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, while still preserving intentional custom aliases and avoiding duplicated labels such as `Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Claude Sonnet 4.6)`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, current PR head `42889570dad4dbb8c6968f5a8bafc8a6e4e4d2a2`, running the actual Agents model-option builder with gateway-model-catalog-shaped entries.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Rebased the PR branch onto current `upstream/main`, ran the focused UI regression suite, then ran a local `node --import tsx` probe against `buildModelOptions()` with configured Anthropic/OpenRouter catalog rows, default aliases, a custom alias, a full-label alias, and duplicate catalog display names.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the current-head local probe:

```text
default alias:
  anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · anthropic
  openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · openrouter
custom alias:
  anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => daily-driver (Claude Sonnet 4.6 · anthropic)
  openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · openrouter

  anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · anthropic
  openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · openrouter
duplicate catalog:
  anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · anthropic
  openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · openrouter
```

Focused tests on current head:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents-utils.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.test.ts
```

- **What was not tested:** No additional gaps.

## Test plan

target.title: fix: guard session reset prompts against fabricated data
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Bare `/new` and `/reset` startup prompts did not explicitly prevent models from presenting invented data as user-provided context.
- Why it matters: On session reset, a model can fabricate realistic calendar/email/JSON-style data and act on it before verification.
- What changed: Added a shared reset-startup guardrail that tells the model not to invent user data and to verify real data with tools before acting.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No runtime behavior, tool permissions, model routing, bootstrap flow, or config defaults changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #75070
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The bare session reset prompt guided startup/greeting behavior but did not explicitly forbid fabricating contextual data during the first reset response.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing prompt tests checked startup and bootstrap wording, but not the anti-fabrication instruction.
- Contributing context (if known): The issue report showed reset responses could present invented calendar/API-style data as real.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session-reset-prompt.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Bare reset prompts, bootstrap-pending reset prompts, and limited-bootstrap reset prompts all include the anti-fabrication guardrail.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is in reset prompt construction, so direct prompt assertions catch regressions without model/provider variability.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing reset prompt tests covered startup/bootstrap wording; this PR extends them.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Bare `/new` and `/reset` startup prompts now explicitly instruct the model not to invent user data and to verify real data with tools before acting.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[/new or /reset] -> [startup prompt] -> [model may fill gaps with fabricated data]

After:
[/new or /reset] -> [startup prompt + anti-fabrication guardrail] -> [model must not present invented data as real]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A, prompt-construction unit coverage
- Integration/channel (if any): Session reset prompt path
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Trigger a bare `/new` or `/reset` session startup.
2. Inspect the generated reset startup prompt.
3. Verify the prompt includes the anti-fabrication guardrail.

### Expected

- Reset startup prompt tells the model not to invent user data and to verify real data with tools before acting.

### Actual

- Previous prompt did not explicitly include that guardrail.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Normal bare reset prompt includes the anti-fabrication guardrail.
  - Bootstrap-pending reset prompt includes the guardrail.
  - Limited-bootstrap reset prompt includes the guardrail.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Current-time prompt behavior remains covered by existing tests.
  - Bootstrap wording remains distinct from normal greeting wording.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Live model behavior across providers.
  - Full repo lint, because `lint:core` currently fails on unrelated existing files.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Prompt wording could become too broad and discourage legitimate startup reads.
  - Mitigation: The guardrail only forbids inventing or presenting unverified data as real; it still allows tool verification before acting.

target.title: feat(feishu): add segment streaming mode
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Feishu streaming delivery mode that accumulates model output and flushes complete segments at pause points, without changing the existing CardKit streaming-card default.

## Motivation

Feishu currently uses `channels.feishu.streaming` for CardKit live streaming. That behavior is useful when users want one live card that updates as the model generates text.

Some workflows need a different delivery style: show completed model text before a tool call, keep tool/status output in order, and then send the follow-up answer after the model resumes. Sending these boundaries as complete segments avoids sentence-by-sentence card updates while still giving users useful progress before the final reply.

## Configuration Behavior

`streaming` remains the top-level enable/disable switch for Feishu streaming delivery. `streamingMode` only applies when `streaming` is enabled.

| `streaming` | `streamingMode` | Behavior                                                                                                                                                |
| ----------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `false`     | `"card"`        | Streaming is disabled. Feishu sends replies through the regular non-streaming path; the mode value is ignored.                                          |
| `false`     | `"segment"`     | Streaming is disabled. Partial text is not accumulated and pause-point segment flushing is not enabled; the mode value is ignored.                      |
| `true`      | `"card"`        | Existing CardKit streaming behavior. Feishu updates one live card and closes it with the final content. This remains the default.                       |
| `true`      | `"segment"`     | New pause-point segment behavior. Feishu accumulates partial text and sends complete segments on tool start, idle, block, tool payload, or final reply. |

`chunkMode` still applies after a reply or segment is ready to send. In segment mode, `streamingMode: "segment"` decides when to flush a semantic segment, then `chunkMode` decides whether that segment needs to be split by length or newline before sending to Feishu.

## Changes

- Add `channels.feishu.streamingMode` with supported values:
  - `"card"`: default, existing CardKit streaming behavior
  - `"segment"`: accumulate model text and flush at pause points
- Preserve current `channels.feishu.streaming` semantics:
  - `true` enables streaming delivery
  - `false` disables streaming delivery
  - default mode remains CardKit streaming
- In segment mode:
  - partial model output is accumulated
  - accumulated text is flushed on tool start, idle, block, tool payload, or final reply
  - duplicate final text after an idle flush is suppressed
  - segment sends are serialized so final/tool payloads cannot overtake an in-flight partial flush
  - each flushed segment independently uses `renderMode: "auto"`
  - mentions are attached only to the first text segment
  - reasoning preview callbacks stay disabled because segment mode does not use live cards
- Update Feishu config schema, generated config metadata, docs, and tests.

## Example

Use segment delivery with:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    feishu: {
      streaming: true,
      streamingMode: "segment",
    },
  },
}
```

Default behavior remains:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    feishu: {
      streaming: true,
      streamingMode: "card",
    },
  },
}
```

## Compatibility

- Existing configs that only set `channels.feishu.streaming` keep the same behavior because `streamingMode` defaults to `"card"`.
- `streaming: false` continues to disable streaming delivery regardless of `streamingMode`.
- Segment mode is opt-in and does not change CardKit streaming-card behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu configs can opt into `streamingMode: "segment"`, and the dispatcher sends one completed segment before a tool pause and a second completed segment after the tool returns.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway connected to a private Feishu DM using the dev Feishu account, running the PR checkout `feishu-segment-streaming-mode-clean` at commit `ad678ae16e9`. Node.js `v22.22.2`; pnpm dependencies installed in `/root/.openclaw/workspace-dev/projects/fork-repos/openclaw`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent this Feishu DM to the dev agent: `First reply with exactly one line: segment-one-ok. Then you must call a tool to check the current date or time. After the tool returns, reply with exactly one line: segment-two-ok.` The dev Feishu account was configured with `streaming: true` and `streamingMode: "segment"`.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime log and copied live output are included below. Gateway journal shows `segment flush reason=toolStart` followed by `segment flush reason=final`; agent session output shows `segment-one-ok`, `toolCall: session_status`, and `segment-two-ok`.
- Observed result after fix: The live Feishu turn emitted `segment-one-ok`, paused for the `session_status` tool call, then emitted `segment-two-ok`. Gateway logs show two segment flushes for the same Feishu turn, first with `reason=toolStart` and then with `reason=final`, and the dispatch completed with `replies=2`.
- What was not tested: Broad multi-user/load behavior and media upload fallback in a real Feishu tenant were not tested in this live run; media fallback behavior is covered by the targeted Feishu dispatcher tests below.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, Feishu had no `streamingMode: "segment"` branch, so this live turn would use the existing CardKit streaming-card/default delivery path instead of two segment flushes.

Copied and redacted gateway journal plus agent-session output from the live Feishu run:

```text
# Redacted OpenClaw gateway journal, 2026-05-06 23:12 Asia/Shanghai
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: received message from <redacted_sender> in <redacted_chat> (p2p)
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: Feishu[dev-bot] DM from <redacted_sender>: First reply with exactly one line: segment-one-ok. Then you must call a tool to check the current date or time. After the tool returns, reply with exactly one line: segment-two-ok.
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: dispatching to agent (session=agent:dev:feishu:direct:<redacted_sender>)
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot] segment flush reason=toolStart mode=text chars=14
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot] segment flush reason=final mode=text chars=14
[feishu] feishu[dev-bot]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=2)

# Redacted agent session transcript for the same Feishu turn
assistant text: segment-one-ok
toolCall: session_status
```

target.title: add option for MCP apps support
target.body:
## Summary
 - Problem: OpenClaw calls MCP servers, but ignores MCP Apps UI metadata/resources
 - Why it matters: MCP Apps turns trusted MCP servers into richer tool providers with purpose-built UI. Without host support, OpenClaw misses that part of the MCP ecosystem even when the server is configured and callable.
- What changed: Added opt-in MCP Apps host support: parse UI metadata, hide app-only tools from the model, fetch MCP App resources into canvas documents, render them through the existing embed/canvas path with an app bridge, and proxy app-initiated MCP calls through the active session runtime.
- What changed: Added restart/reload hardening for restored app views, wider assistant canvas layout, docs, config schema support,tests, and a security-audit warning when `mcp.apps.enabled=true`.
-  What did NOT change (scope boundary): Normal MCP tool execution still works without MCP Apps. MCP Apps are default-off behind `mcp.apps.enabled`; this does not add a marketplace, new MCP server examples, or plugin-only architecture.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- MCP app-only tools are not materialized for the model.
- MCP Apps UI payloads are only fetched/rendered when `mcp.apps.enabled=true`.
- App bridge calls are rejected when MCP Apps are disabled.
- App bridge calls enforce app-callable tool visibility.
- Persisted/restored app views can recreate an MCP runtime after gateway restart.
- Existing `[embed]` canvas rendering remains the fallback path when MCP Apps metadata is absent or disabled.

- Target test or file:
  - New opt-in config: `mcp.apps.enabled`.
  - Default behavior is unchanged for normal MCP tools; MCP Apps UI rendering is disabled by default.
  - When enabled, trusted MCP servers can render interactive app views in chat using the existing canvas/embed path.
  - MCP Apps views may call app-visible MCP tools through the active session runtime.
  - App-only MCP tools are hidden from the model.
  - `openclaw security audit` now warns when MCP Apps are enabled.
  - Docs now describe MCP Apps setup, scope, and security considerations. 
- Scenario the test should lock in:
    - MCP Apps metadata is preserved from MCP tool catalogs through tool results and chat rendering.
    - App-only tools are hidden from the model.
    - Model-only tools are blocked from app bridge calls.
    - MCP Apps UI rendering and bridge proxy calls require `mcp.apps.enabled=true`.
    - Restored app views can recreate an MCP runtime after gateway restart.
    - Existing canvas/embed rendering remains the fallback when MCP Apps are disabled or metadata is absent.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
    - The feature crosses runtime materialization, gateway proxying, config/bootstrap, and UI rendering. Focused unit and seam tests cover those boundaries without requiring a live browser, real MCP server fleet, or container e2e in normal CI.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
    - Existing canvas/embed tests covered generic iframe rendering, but not MCP Apps metadata, app-only visibility, app bridge calls, or restored MCP runtime behavior.

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes
  - New opt-in config: `mcp.apps.enabled`.
  - Default behavior is unchanged for normal MCP tools; MCP Apps UI rendering is disabled by default.
  - When enabled, trusted MCP servers can render interactive app views in chat using the existing canvas/embed path.
  - MCP Apps views may call app-visible MCP tools through the active session runtime.
  - App-only MCP tools are hidden from the model.
  - `openclaw security audit` now warns when MCP Apps are enabled.
  - Docs now describe MCP Apps setup, scope, and security considerations.
  
## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```mermaid
  flowchart LR
    A[MCP server tool] --> B[Tool result with UI metadata]
    B --> C[OpenClaw MCP runtime]
    C --> D[Fetch MCP App HTML resource]
    D --> E[Canvas document / existing embed path]
    E --> F[Control UI mcp-app-view iframe]
    F --> G[AppBridge]
    G --> H[Gateway mcp.* proxy]
    H --> C
  ```
## Security Impact (required)
  - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): Yes
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
  - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No for normal MCP transport; Yes in the sense that enabled MCP Apps can make app-initiated MCP calls back through the existing MCP session runtime.
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
  - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
  - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
    - Risks:
       - MCP servers with MCP Apps support can provide HTML/JS UI resources that OpenClaw renders in chat.
       -  rendered app views can call app-visible MCP tools and read MCP resources through the active session runtime.
       - app views receive the tool input/result payload they are rendering.
    - Mitigations:
        - MCP Apps are default-off behind `mcp.apps.enabled=true`.
        - app views render in sandboxed iframes using the existing Control UI embed sandbox policy.
        - app-only tools are hidden from the model, and model-only tools are blocked from app bridge calls.
        - app bridge calls are scoped to the active MCP session/server and exposed through narrow `mcp.*` gateway methods.
        - `openclaw security audit` warns when MCP Apps are enabled.
- Trust model: enabling MCP Apps means trusting the configured MCP servers not only as tool providers, but also as UI code providers.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment
  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: local OpenClaw container via Podman
  - Model/provider: Claude Sonnet 4.6
  - Integration/channel (if any): Control UI chat with configured MCP servers
  - Relevant config (redacted):

```json
  {
    "mcp": {
      "apps": {
        "enabled": true
      },
      "servers": {
        "system-monitor": {
          "command": "npx",
          "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-system-monitor", "--stdio"]
        },
        "pdf": {
          "command": "npx",
          "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-pdf", "--stdio"]
        },
        "qr": {
          "url": "http://127.0.0.1:3108/mcp",
          "transport": "streamable-http"
        }
      }
    }
  }
```

### Steps
  1. Build and run an OpenClaw image from this branch.
  2. Enable MCP Apps with openclaw config set mcp.apps.enabled true --strict-json.
  3. Configure the system-monitor, pdf, and qr MCP servers.
  4. Start the QR MCP sidecar if using the QR example.
  5. Start a fresh chat/session in the Control UI.
  6. Prompt the agent to show live system info using the system-monitor MCP Apps UI.
  7. Prompt the agent to generate a QR code using the QR MCP Apps UI.
  8. Prompt the agent to display https://arxiv.org/pdf/1706.03762 using the PDF MCP Apps UI.
  9. Restart the OpenClaw container and reload the Control UI.
  10. Confirm the persisted PDF app view reloads and reconnects.

### Expected

  - Normal MCP tools still work.
  - With mcp.apps.enabled=true, MCP Apps UI resources render as interactive embedded app views.
  - System monitor renders a live monitor UI.
  - QR renders an embedded QR UI.
  - PDF renders an interactive PDF viewer.
  - Restored app views can reconnect after gateway/container restart.
  - With mcp.apps.enabled=false, app bridge calls are rejected and MCP Apps render through the normal fallback path instead of the app bridge.

### Actual

  - Verified locally with system-monitor, QR, and PDF MCP Apps examples.
  - Verified upstream image comparison: upstream can call the MCP servers but does not render the MCP Apps UI resources.
  - Verified restart/reload behavior for the PDF app view.

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aaa93975-4406-493e-a060-4ac64763704b

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
    - No migration is required for existing installs. MCP Apps support is default-off.
    - To enable it, set:
      ```bash
      openclaw config set mcp.apps.enabled true --strict-json
      ```
    - Configure MCP servers that expose MCP Apps / `mcp-ui` resources.
    - Restart OpenClaw so the updated config is loaded.

## Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: Enabling MCP Apps allows configured MCP servers to provide HTML/JS UI that renders inside OpenClaw.
    - Mitigation: Feature is opt-in via `mcp.apps.enabled`; app UI renders in a sandboxed iframe; docs and security audit call out the trust boundary.
  - Risk: App views can call app-visible MCP tools/resources through the bridge, increasing the practical surface area of a trusted MCP server.
    - Mitigation: Bridge is scoped to MCP app methods only; model-only tools are blocked from app bridge calls; app-only tools are hidden from the model.
  - Risk: Restored chat views may need to reconnect to MCP runtimes after a container restart.
    - Mitigation: Gateway proxy lazily recreates the session MCP runtime from config when an app bridge call arrives and no runtime is currently active.
  - Risk: Servers without MCP Apps support, or installs with the feature disabled, should not regress.
    - Mitigation: Existing MCP tool execution path remains unchanged; UI falls back to the existing embed/canvas rendering behavior when MCP Apps are disabled or metadata is absent.
